persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: any same-name relative could in principle share the prisons post. Why it fails: 'de Wilton' is unique in the entire corpus (1 staff-list official, 1 biography), the rank 'Major' and initials 'A. W.' agree, and the stint reproduces the bio's exact 1905 appointment 'inspr.-gen. of prisons and supt. of convict establishment, Ceylon' as 'Inspector-General of Prisons and Superintendent of the Colombo Convict Establishments', held continuously 1905-1922 with 14 edition-years co-occurring. There is no plausible alternative holder.

evidence_cited.fact: inspr.-gen. of prisons and supt. of convict establishment, Ceylon, 15th July, 1905
Inspector-General of Prisons and Superintendent of the Colombo Convict Establishments

persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second de Wilton (e.g. son) holding the same Ceylon prisons office.

notes: Unique surname, matching rank and initials, verbatim specific post, 14 edition cooccurrences.
